props.message:
POSTED LAW

1. Visitors check in with the sheriff.
2. No torches in the dry months.
3. We mind our own. We expect the same.

WARNING

A bone knight wanders the outer edge.
The sheriff hasn't dealt with it yet.
Keep your distance and your wits.

MEMORIAL

For those who came to the frontier
and did not come back.

Names carved here:
Kell. Mar. Old Bren. Two children
whose names are still painted on the
porch beside their mother's door.

WARNING

The rafters of the lower hall
have a bat. We know.
It eats well. Leave it.

POSTED

Water from the south well only.
The north well has gone bitter.
It has been bitter since the
Drawing-In passed by.

NOTICE

New arrivals from the Drift welcome.
Quiet preferred. Most of us
came here to be left alone
and we extend the courtesy.

EDGE OF TOWN

Beyond this point: scrub, wind,
and whatever Khorgaul's grip has left.
Walk armed. Walk back.

props.open_message: A small iron key. Heavier than it looks. The Locals don't lock much, but what they lock, they mean.
A Piece of Heart. Tucked in a settler's spare-room chest, kept for travelers.

root.speaker: Sheriff Brun
root.text: Hold a moment, walker. New face. State your business in the Locals.

pass.speaker: Sheriff Brun
pass.text: *(considers)* Fair enough. Pass through, then. Mind the outer edge — there's a bone knight wandering. We'll deal with it. Eventually.

help.speaker: Sheriff Brun
help.text: *(softens, just a little)* That's kindly said. Most don't. If you've a sword, the outer edge wants one. The knight there isn't kin to anything we know.

trouble.speaker: Sheriff Brun
trouble.text: Bone knight on the rim. Bat in the rafters of the lower hall — leave that one, it eats the moths. The knight, though. He paces. He doesn't sleep. Khorgaul's grip on him.

frontier.speaker: Sheriff Brun
frontier.text: Wood and stone. Wind and watch. We came out here because the Sprawl was too loud and the Burrows too many. We mind our own. We bury our own. Ask no more than that.

root.speaker: Settler Marn
root.text: Walker. Mind the porch — the boards are honest, the nails less so.

life.speaker: Settler Marn
life.text: Hard wood. Hard winter. Good wife, loud child. We mend, we mind, we make do. Wouldn't trade it for the Sprawl's racket.

root.speaker: Settler Hess
root.text: Walker. Don't mind the small one. He's been at the knight talk all morning.

knight.speaker: Settler Hess
knight.text: Wants to be one. Wants the sword, the cloak, the lot. I tell him knights bury more than they save. He doesn't hear me. Children rarely do.

root.speaker: Little Tem
root.text: ARE YOU A KNIGHT? You look like a knight. Sort of. Smaller. ARE YOU? I'm going to be a knight! I have a stick! Come SEE my stick!

two.speaker: Little Tem
two.text: Mama says knights bury more than they save and Papa says don't say that to the boy and I say I'm going to bury the BAD ones. The bone ones. With my STICK. Pew. Pew. PEW.

root.speaker: Refugee Ema
root.text: Walker. You've a kind step. Ask, if you would.

drift.speaker: Refugee Ema
drift.text: The Drift. The festival town. Where every morning is a new morning, and the day before never quite happened. ...I lived there a long time. Longer than I knew.

why.speaker: Refugee Ema
why.text: *(quiet)* I couldn't bear forgetting. The faces of those I'd known went pale at dawn. I came here because the Locals remember. Even the bitter things. *Especially* the bitter things.

bye.speaker: Refugee Ema
bye.text: Walk well. Remember what you can.

root.speaker: tar
root.text: I bundle things together. Hearts, shafts, powder — and a wisp in a jar, on a lucky week. Bundle, unbundle. Same coin.

fare.speaker: tar
fare.text: Better than yesterday. Worse than the year before. The Locals haul, I pack. That's the bargain — flags `czf` for me, `xzf` for the next walker who needs it.

wares.speaker: tar
wares.text: Full archive — hearts, arrows, bombs, the rare bottled fairy. And `unzip`, if you keep finding caches you can't pry open.

bye.speaker: tar
bye.text: Walk well. Come back lighter, if you can.

root.speaker: Nano
root.text:
*soft, small voice*

I just want to write something simple. The keys are listed at the bottom. ^O to save. ^X to leave. I do not understand why anyone needs more than this.

root.speaker: Arch-User
root.text:
*before you can speak, he speaks*

I built my home from the source. Every beam I cut. Every nail I forged. Every shingle I split. I do not let anyone forget this.

good.speaker: Arch-User
good.text: It is exactly the home I wanted, because I built it. *he beams* You should build yours from the source. It is the only way.

bye.speaker: Arch-User
bye.text: *he watches you go, ready to tell the next walker*
